headline: How Halliburton’s Vaca Muerta Electric Fracturing Deal with YPF Will Impact Halliburton (HAL) Investors
summary: Earlier this week, Halliburton announced it had secured an exclusive, multi-year multibillion-dollar contract from YPF to deliver bundled unconventional completions services in Argentina’s Vaca Muerta shale, including the first international deployment of its ZEUS electric fracturing and OCTIV Auto Frac digital services. This agreement underscores how Halliburton is applying electrification, automation, and advanced digital workflows to support efficiency gains and emissions-intensity...

This agreement underscores how Halliburton is applying electrification, automation, and advanced digital workflows to support efficiency gains and emissions-intensity...

headline: Halliburton price target raised to $45 from $38 at Citi
summary: Citi analyst Scott Gruber raised the firm’s price target on Halliburton (HAL) to $45 from $38 and keeps a Buy rating on the shares. The firm adjusted targets in the oil and gas equipment and services group as part of a Q1 preview. The sector has “cross-currents” with the completion market recovering and companies with Middle East exposure brining uncertainty, the analyst tells investors in a research note.Claim 55% Off TipRanksUnlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter
